name: Cashew Chicken
description: The Woks of Life's restaurant-style Cantonese cashew chicken — velveted chicken stir-fried with bell pepper, water chestnuts, scallions, and roasted cashews in a glossy honey-hoisin-soy sauce. Serve over steamed rice.
recipeIngredient: 1 lb boneless skinless chicken breast, cut into 1-inch pieces
1/2 cup red bell pepper, chopped (bump to 1 whole pepper for more veg)
1/2 cup water chestnuts, cut into 1/2-inch pieces
1 cup unsalted cashews, roasted at 350F for 5 minutes
2 Tbsp cornstarch mixed with 2 Tbsp water (slurry)
recipeInstructions: Velvet the chicken: mix with cornstarch, 3 Tbsp water, oil, and oyster sauce until the liquid is absorbed. Combine all the sauce ingredients in a separate bowl. Have everything ready before firing up the wok.
Heat a wok over high heat until smoking. Add 2 Tbsp oil around the perimeter. Cook the marinated chicken about 1 minute per side until ~80% cooked; transfer to a bowl.
Add the remaining 1 Tbsp oil with the ginger; fry 5 seconds. Add garlic, then immediately the bell pepper, scallions, and water chestnuts. Stir-fry 30 seconds.
Pour the Shaoxing wine around the wok perimeter; stir-fry 10 seconds.
Pour in the prepared sauce, deglazing the wok sides. Add the cooked chicken and its juices, bring to a simmer, then add the roasted cashews.
Stir the cornstarch slurry and add about three-quarters; simmer, stirring, until the sauce thickens and coats evenly. Adjust thickness to preference.
Plate and serve immediately over steamed rice.
author: The Woks of Life

ai.cliffNotes: Velveted chicken + a balanced honey/hoisin/soy sauce + roasted cashews, stir-fried fast in a screaming-hot wok and served over rice. A complete one-pan meal with the pepper, water chestnuts, and scallions as the veg.
keyTips: Velveting is the tenderness secret — work the cornstarch + 3 Tbsp water into the chicken until the liquid is fully absorbed; that extra water keeps the breast moist and tender even after high-heat stir-frying.
Roast the cashews at 350F for 5 minutes before they go in — toasting deepens their flavor and keeps them crunchy in the sauce.
The sauce is built on hoisin + honey balanced by a little rice vinegar — the acid cuts the richness so it doesn't read as cloying. Mix it in a bowl ahead so you can pour it in fast.

Mise en place is everything here: the whole stir-fry is over in a couple of minutes once the wok is hot, so have the chicken, aromatics, veg, sauce, and slurry all prepped and within reach before you start.
Add the cornstarch slurry gradually (about three-quarters first) and judge the thickness — you can always add more, but you can't thin a gluey sauce.
ai.source: Claude (prose-mined from The Woks of Life article)
